Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to analyze each case against the normative process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The normative description process starts with Receive order then Check inventory If inventory sufficient Authorize payment then Package items then Ship order then Close order If insufficient Cancel order then Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key constraints Authorize payment must occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items must occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order must occur after packaging and never after cancellation Close order final

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Also no cancellation after fulfillment activities The description says after cancellation it would be illogical to continue with packaging or shipping so cancellation should be followed directly by close order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive Check Authorize Package Ship Close This matches the sufficient inventory path perfectly No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive Check Package Authorize Ship Close Note Package items occurs before Authorize payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The normative says Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Here packaging is before authorization That violates the order authorization must come before packaging

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging here authorize occurs after packaging so it is after packaging not before

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So case 2 violates

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive Check Authorize Cancel Ship Close

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: After Check inventory they did Authorize payment Then Cancel order The normative If inventory insufficient after Check should go directly to Cancel But here they authorized payment first which suggests they thought inventory sufficient But then canceled

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: If inventory is found to be sufficient then authorize payment So if it is insufficient you should not authorize payment Authorizing payment when inventory insufficient is a deviation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: After cancellation they did Ship order The description says Once an order is canceled it would be illogical to continue with activities intended for fulfillment such as packaging or shipping So shipping after cancellation is a violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also they never packaged items but they shipped

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So case 3 has multiple violations after cancel should be close not ship

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive Check Cancel Close This matches the insufficient inventory path perfectly No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive Check Authorize Ship Close Missing Package items

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The normative says after authorization should package items then ship Here they skipped packaging and went straight to ship

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: That violates Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping and Ship order should only occur after packaging

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thus cases 2 3 and 5 violate Case 1 and 4 are correct
